mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or the rank correlation between rate changes and returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals extreme labor market tightness that forces the central bank to pause rate hikes, triggering a short-term equity relief rally over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is negative in over 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 3.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in existing home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle that reduces consumer wealth and durable goods spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in over 55% of expansion periods, or the predictive coefficient flips sign during NBER-defined expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290, markets anticipate restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in over 50% of observations, or the rank correlation between payroll changes and returns is consistently negative over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP coinciding with falling existing home sales signals a broad demand shock that reduces corporate cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production combined with stable unemployment signals robust supply-side growth without labor market overheating, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during mid-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day SPX returns in over 60% of mid-cycle observations, or the predictive power vanishes when tested exclusively on NBER-defined mid-cycle periods.
